topic: Two friends both apply for the same job
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the office hummed, a sound that seemed amplified in the silence of my small apartment. I stared at the application form, the ink blurring slightly under my gaze. Across the desk, Liam was already packing his briefcase, a confident ease about him radiating off him like heat. I could feel my palms growing slick against the glossy paper. Every time I glanced at his neatly organized portfolio, a tightness coiled in my chest, making it hard to draw a full breath. *This is it,* I thought, a cold knot forming beneath my ribs. I picked up my pen, the tip trembling almost imperceptibly, and started writing, the words feeling heavy and inadequate.

[story 2]

"Are you sure about this, Maya?" Liam asked, leaning back in his chair, his expression open and reassuring. He patted my shoulder, a gesture that felt both comforting and strangely isolating. I forced a smile, the muscles in my face feeling stiff. My throat constricted as I swallowed, the dryness in my mouth making the action difficult. I looked down at the printed list of requirements, the fine print seeming to mock my lack of experience in this specific area. My hands, resting on the desk, started to sweat, and I quickly wiped them on my jeans, acutely aware of the sudden, embarrassing dampness.

[story 3]

The shared coffee shop felt too loud today. I kept catching Liam’s eye from across the table; he was laughing easily with a colleague, his posture relaxed and unburdened. My own shoulders hunched inward, a physical shrinking that seemed to pull my entire frame closer to the chair. When I finally opened the application, the paper felt alien in my hands. My heart began to beat a frantic rhythm against my ribs, a rapid, uneven drumming that I was certain everyone at the table could hear. I gripped my mug so tightly my knuckles turned white, trying desperately to project an image of calm competence that felt miles away from the churning uncertainty inside me.
